Background:
thumb|left|Himawari as a toddler.
Himawari is the second and youngest child of Naruto and Hinata Uzumaki, born six years after the Fourth Shinobi World War and two years after her brother, Boruto. As her parents continued to stay close to their own childhood friends, she ultimately got to know the kids of her parents' respective friends very well. In the anime, she appears to be close with Sarada Uchiha, who calls her "Hima-chan".
On the day that Naruto was to be inaugurated as the Seventh Hokage, Himawari wanted to bring her panda toy to the ceremony, but Boruto, fearing that he would end up carrying it, tried to take it away from her. When the toy's head was ripped off in the ensuing tug-of-war, Himawari awoke her Byakugan and attacked Boruto in her anger, despite his vehement apologies. Naruto, sensing her killing intent, arrived and shielded Boruto, though he was knocked out in the process. Fearful of what an attack that could defeat his father would do to him, Boruto tried to run from Himawari, but she quickly located her brother hiding in a closet. From then on, he vowed to never anger her again.
Personality:
Himawari is a cheerful and considerate girl. This also extends to strangers, such as when she gave up her much-desired Kurama toy to a boy, believing that he needed it more than her. Likewise, she shows a strong affinity towards animals, able to approach most wild beasts calmly and with acceptance from them. She is quite considerate about her own future; while many expect her to become a kunoichi due to her lineage, she has decided to experience various career paths to determine her own goals, a choice her mother supports and encourages since she didn't had the same opportuny growing up.. She has picked up her father's trait of referring to others with nicknames, such as when she referred to Orochimaru as "Oro-chan". 
Although she is very mature and responsible for her age, Himawari has a temper, becoming violent when provoked, much like her grandmother Kushina. For example, when her favourite plush was accidentally damaged by Boruto, she assaulted him. She usually has no memory of these outbursts, but this display of aggression made her elder brother run away and hide from her and vow never to anger her again. The same event also caused her father and Kurama to be terrified of her. As mentioned by Iruka Umino, she possesses the same stubbornness that her father and brother possess, while also being somewhat boisterous and hyperactive, mainly when she's determined in achieving a certain goal. She also shows signs of her father's nindō of never giving up at the face of difficulty, as seen when she urged Ehō Norimaki to continue their Academy trial session despite them being trapped in a well. However, contrary to Boruto, she is also shown to display the same traits of patience and humbleness of her grandfather Minato, being able to remain calm under dangerous situations and push herself to help others. She is also very forgiving, able to acknowledge peoples' change when they make the effort to do the right thing.
Himawari is shown to adore her family. She hopes to make her late cousin Neji happy by bringing flowers to his grave, a scene which touched her mother's heart. She often helps her mother with chores and, when her mother was injured during Momoshiki's attack, she stayed by her bedside as she was healed by Sakura, whom she refers as her "aunt" out of fondness for her friendship with her parents. She is mostly understanding of her often-absent father and his Hokage duties, even after he missed her birthday, but yearns for him to be home.
Abilities:
Well before training as a ninja, similar to her older brother, Himawari has shown considerable potential and prodigious talent as a result of her Uzumaki-Hyūga bloodlines. Even more, her untrained might as a child could terrify her brother and knock out her very powerful father with a single blow. Since entering the Academy, she quickly became a standout among her fellow students for her performance. Following her new status as a jinchūriki, her immense potential excited even Daemon, whose might exceeds the full-powered Code. While accessing her new power, she briefly overwhelmed Jura, the leader and strongest of the Human God Trees, forcing him to use actual effort for the first time in his life to defeat her.
[Byakugan] thumb|left|Himawari's Byakugan.
At a very young age, from her Hyūga heritage, Himawari awakened the Byakugan in anger when her favourite toy was torn. Hiashi noted how impressive it was, especially with no prior training. When activated, her normally blue eyes take on the white and featureless appearance of the Byakugan with the veins on her temples bulging. With it, she possesses a near 360° field of x-ray vision (except for a small blind spot at the upper thoracic vertebrae). She can also see through solid surfaces, across great distances, and clearly perceive the chakra pathway system and locate the tenketsu. Without prior training, she could quickly make effective use of it. At the same time, she has originally had no conscious control over her Byakugan's activation, only activating involuntarily from moments of extreme emotional build-up. By age 10, she learned to access it at will, though struggles to maintain it for long periods.
[Physical and Chakra Prowess] thumb|Himawari knocks out her father.
As a descendant of the Uzumaki, Himawari inherited a powerful life-force and chakra. She has very advanced chakra control  for her age. With no prior training, she could perform Tree Climbing Practice on her first try and only failed from losing focus. In addition, Himawari has deceptive raw might, able to launch large amounts of scrap metal with a single strike and jump a considerable distance into the air. She likewise could move too fast for Boruto to follow. Naturally, she has remarkable agility and athleticism, easily completing an Academy training course and beating other children who already had trained rigorously.
